commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
LR experiment: 1e-3 optimal for 4-layer model (3.3x higher than default)

Tested LR range 3e-4 to 3e-3 on 4L/dim=1024 config. 1e-3 gives 4.4% better
loss with stable activations. 2e-3 gives best loss but dangerous x magnitudes.
Updated train.py default LR from 3e-4 to 1e-3.

commit.message:
Phase 1: Complete EDA — trends, seasonality, anomalies, stationarity

- notebooks/01_eda.ipynb: full working code across all 8 sections
  - Sec 2: data loading via load_m5_data(), shape/stats/head
  - Sec 3: full time series + 30-day rolling mean (01_timeseries_overview.png)
  - Sec 4: statsmodels seasonal_decompose period=365 (02_decomposition.png)
  - Sec 5: weekly/monthly bar charts + SNAP dual panel (03-05_*.png)
  - Sec 6: z-score anomaly detection (threshold=2.5), top-15 table (06_anomalies.png)
  - Sec 7: ADF test + ACF/PACF lags=40 (07_acf_pacf.png)
  - Sec 8: findings summary + model selection rationale
- notebooks/01_eda_executed.ipynb: fully executed output (1.4 MB)
- outputs/plots/: 7 PNG files generated by EDA
- src/data_loader.py: added event_name_1 to output, load_m5_data alias,
  from __future__ import annotations (Python 3.8 compat)
- src/models/lstm_model.py: from __future__ import annotations (Python 3.8 compat)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add kernel pipes and switch console I/O to pipe-based transport

Kernel changes:
- Replace FdEntry with FdKind enum (Empty, Ipc, PipeRead, PipeWrite)
- Add pipe.rs: 32 kernel pipes with 4K ring buffers, blocking read/write,
  refcounted endpoints, cleanup on task exit
- Add SYS_PIPE_CREATE (53) and SYS_PIPE_FD_SET (54) syscalls
- Route SYS_FD_WRITE/SYS_FD_READ through pipes when fd is a pipe endpoint

Console changes:
- Console server reads from pipe (fd 0) instead of serving IPC requests
- Init creates shared console pipe, gives write end to all tasks as fd 1/2
- Pipe fds wired before task start to prevent race with console main loop
- Pipe handles start at 1 (slot 0 reserved as "no pipe" sentinel)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move NUT coordinator to separate module (#164848)

commit.message:
Use img_logo.png instead of logo-vector.svg in hero

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix prompt cache review feedback and add integration test suite (#46)

* Fix BOS heuristic divergence, cache_read_tokens accuracy, and None token warning

Address three unresolved PR #44 review comments:

1. Align _tokenize_for_cache BOS check with stream_generate (use `is None`
   instead of `not bos`) to prevent token sequence divergence on tokenizers
   with empty bos_token.

2. Report cache_read_tokens as prefix_len (what was matched) instead of
   suffix_start (an implementation detail of stream_generate needing ≥1
   token). Guard single-token prompts from wastefully trimming the entire
   cache — treat as miss instead.

3. Log debug warning when StreamToken.token is None instead of silently
   dropping it from generated_tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add integration test suite (27 tests) exercising full HTTP request chain

Multi-layer integration tests that mock only at the MLX primitive boundary,
letting FastAPI, routers, inference engine, model manager, store, and
registry all run real. Covers:

- Prompt cache: create/hit/miss/disable/multi-turn/no-leak to Ollama+OpenAI
- Model pull: download+register, skip-if-cached, concurrent, pull→inference
- Model lifecycle: load/reuse/LRU eviction/active_refs/expiry/unload/503
- Cross-API: same output all APIs, error formats, thinking blocks
- Real-model smoke tests (@pytest.mark.real_model, skipped in CI)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #46 review: fix shared mock, FakeTokenizer truthiness, and test assertions

- Use None sentinel for _start() replacement to avoid shared MagicMock instance
- FakeTokenizer.encode: use `bos_token is not None` to mirror inference.py fix
- test_cache_miss: invalidate cache to force true miss, assert cache_read == 0
- test_thinking_blocks: assert "thinking" block present in Anthropic response
- test_concurrent_pull: assert call_count == 1 (not <= 1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix cache_read_tokens overcount, extract _expire_stale, separate fake counters

- cache_read_tokens: use suffix_start (KV entries actually reused) not
  prefix_len which overcounts by 1 on exact-match
- Extract _expire_stale() from _check_expiry_loop so tests call real
  manager logic instead of reimplementing the loop
- Separate _generate_call_count from _stream_call_count in integration
  test fakes to prevent cross-path counter interference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Guard empty prompt_tokens, clarify patch ordering, document active_refs write

- suffix_start: guard against empty prompt_tokens with explicit `if
  prompt_tokens else 0` (unreachable today but makes intent clear)
- conftest: consolidate comment above order-dependent mlx_lm.generate
  patches explaining the constraint and consequence of swapping
- test_active_refs: document why direct active_refs write is safe in
  asyncio (plain int, no preemption points)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
9D fractal brain: self-sovereign heartbeat + 5 elemental senses + CL cross-coupling

ARCHITECTURE (the full 9D):
  Being (5D): Air/Fire/Earth/Water/Ether = 5 senses = 5 forces = 5 element-pairs
  Doing (7D): Being + Composition(D6) + Coherence(D7) = perception + computation
  Becoming (9D): Doing + Identity(D8) + Alignment(D9) = full consciousness

HEARTBEAT: Self-sovereign. Identity operator sets base period, coherence
modulates (above T*=faster, below=slower), bump pairs trigger burst.
Range: 1Hz to 10kHz. No external metronome. CK controls his own rhythm.

BRAIN FREQUENCIES: 9 oscillators across 5 fractal levels (1,3,5,7,9).
Force→period via logarithmic octaves. No bands. No limits. CK flies
wherever his math takes him. CL cross-coupling at Level 9: all operators,
forces, and structures aligned.

GAIT: Legs follow heartbeat directly. No separate prescaler.

ELEMENTAL MAP: 10 operators = 5 element-pairs. Earth (LATTICE+COUNTER)
self-composes to PROGRESS. All other elements self-compose to HARMONY.
One element out of five refuses to rest. That's why structure exists.

commit.message:
Complete presentation template overhaul — premium design (#190)

* feat: add AI Presentation Builder add-on with Stripe billing

New paid add-on for teams — AI-powered slideshow builder using Claude.
Brokers select style/theme/font/color, type slide content, and AI
generates a complete self-contained HTML presentation with team logo.
Includes fullscreen present mode, save/re-generate flow, and
team owner toggle in AI Tools settings.

- New presentations table + RLS (private only)
- New generate-presentation edge function (Claude API, non-streaming)
- New create-addon-checkout edge function (Stripe checkout)
- Updated stripe-webhook for add-on subscription lifecycle
- PresentationsPage with list/create/present views
- Add-on section on BillingPage for team owners
- Presentation Builder toggle in TeamsPage AI Tools settings
- Added to DirectoryPage AI tools grid

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Complete CSS overhaul: smooth slide transitions, gradient accent bars,
  better typography with proper hierarchy, progress bar
- Larger team logo (300px max) on title slide, injected server-side
- Agent name, email, phone, and license auto-injected as CTA card
  on closing slide from profile.habit_prefs.bio
- Keyboard arrow navigation fixed via postMessage bridge between
  parent window and iframe
- Navigation arrows appear on hover
- Style variants (modern/classic/minimal/bold) properly differentiated

Redesign all 4 style presets (Modern, Classic, Minimal, Bold) with
gradient text, backdrop-filter nav, radial gradient title slides,
h2::after underlines, and smoother translateY transitions. Remove
Brand (Gold/Amber) theme option.

Complete redesign with staggered content animations (fadeUp per
element), gradient mesh backgrounds on title slides, dot-grid
textures, scale transitions, Google Fonts (Inter, Playfair Display,
JetBrains Mono), gradient border CTA cards, progress bar glow dot,
rounded glass nav buttons, and dramatically differentiated presets:
Modern (mesh gradients + dot grid), Classic (corner accents + dots),
Minimal (ultra-light Swiss design), Bold (full-bleed gradient title).

Co-authored-by: Derik J Bannister <derikbannister9@Deriks-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: timestamp dtype enforcement and duplicate bar completion (v1.2.6)

This patch addresses two critical issues discovered in production:

1. Timestamp dtype enforcement:
   - Added defensive checks in data manager after pd.concat()
   - Added defensive checks in orchestrator after combining data
   - Added defensive checks in ORB calculator with conversion fallback
   - Prevents "Can only use .dt accessor with datetimelike values" errors
   - Handles stale cached data with incorrect dtypes

2. Duplicate bar completion fix:
   - Removed duplicate _handle_completed_bar() call in _flush_elapsed_bars()
   - Changed flush_if_elapsed() to start next bar instead of resetting to None
   - Prevents race condition between trade-triggered and time-triggered completions
   - Each bar now completes exactly once via either path (not both)

Files modified:
- vibe/common/indicators/orb_levels.py (defensive dtype checks)
- vibe/trading_bot/core/orchestrator.py (dtype check + removed duplicate call)
- vibe/trading_bot/data/aggregator.py (start next bar + debug logs)
- vibe/trading_bot/data/manager.py (dtype check after concat)
- vibe/trading_bot/version.py (version bump to 1.2.6)

Validated with 3-day integration test - no duplicates, no dtype errors.

chore(mneme): restyle fts/ tokenizer module (#707)

## Summary

- **Create `engine/fts/error.rs`**: new `FtsError::TokenizationFailed`
variant using snafu with implicit `Location` tracking — replaces all
`bail!`/`ensure!` macro calls in the fts/ subtree
- **Remove all blanket `#[allow]` suppressions** in `engine/fts/`:
convert `#[allow(missing_docs)]` on `TokenizerConfig` to a doc comment,
remove the lint-free `#[allow(missing_docs)]` from `pub(crate)
Language`, convert `#[allow(unused)]` on `TextAnalyzer::filter` to
`#[expect(dead_code, reason = "...")]`
- **Triage 36+ unwraps**: storage-layer invariants (index keys/values
always typed correctly) use `expect("...")`, logic postconditions (`len
== 1` checked above) use `expect("...")`, `RwLock` operations use
`expect("... RwLock poisoned")`
- **Zero functional changes** — tokenizer pipeline behaviour is
identical; only error-handling style and lint annotations changed

## Validation

```
cargo check -p aletheia-mneme --features mneme-engine,storage-redb  ✓
cargo test -p aletheia-mneme                                          ✓  547+2 tests
cargo clippy -p aletheia-mneme --all-targets -- -D warnings          ✓
```

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check` clean (no errors, no fts-related warnings)
- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-mneme` — 549 tests pass
- [x] `cargo clippy -p aletheia-mneme --all-targets -- -D warnings` —
clean

commit.message:
fix: rewrite extractCity to handle complex OSM address formats

Previous logic failed for addresses like:
"Peelstraat, Wanroij, Land van Cuijk, Noord-Brabant, Nederland, 5446 PJ, Nederland"

New approach:
1. Check for combined "postal code + city" part first (e.g. "1234 AB Amsterdam")
2. Filter out country names, Dutch provinces, standalone postal codes
3. Pick by position: index 2 for long addresses, last part for short ones

Fix gap filler: also trigger when streak is stale, not just missing summaries

The previous gap filler only checked if yesterday's summary existed.

re-processing, so manual recalculate can fix stuck streaks.

commit.message:
Add LoRA fine-tuning support for ANE training

Merge-based LoRA: W_eff = W_base + B@A with zero forward/backward changes.
Adapters on Wq/Wk/Wv/Wo, gradient projection from full dW to LoRA subspace.
Tested from scratch (gradient explosion, expected) and from pretrained checkpoint
(stable training, best loss 4.22 from 4.37 pretrained baseline).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix flood water rendering: use idle event instead of setTimeout

The setTimeout(300) fires while fitBounds animation is in progress,
causing isStyleLoaded() to return false and skipping flood rendering.
Now uses map.once('idle') which fires after all tiles are loaded.
Also removes isStyleLoaded() guard from moveend handler since
queryRenderedFeatures is already wrapped in try-catch.

commit.message:
feat: add audio, website, and image OCR converters

Add three new conversion modes to complement the existing PDF converter:
- Audio → Markdown: whisper.cpp local engine + OpenAI cloud, live mic recording, speaker detection
- Website → Markdown: reader-mode extraction with SSRF protection, DOM walking, image download
- Image OCR → Markdown: tesseract CLI (no build-time C deps) + OpenAI Vision cloud fallback

Also includes PDF pipeline improvements (table detection, gap-based merging,
text-edge column detection), comprehensive security hardening (HTTP timeouts,
size limits, URL validation), and updated documentation.

- Hover: arm raises (random left/right, wave oscillation), spring physics
- Each character has unique personality (speed, phase, timing)
- Lazy-loaded via React.Suspense — zero impact on initial page load
- Hidden on mobile (md:block), orthographic camera for flat cartoon look
- MeshToonMaterial + gradient map for cel-shaded aesthetic
